segments.text: Why does every hospital in the world use
this weird bean-shaped tray? It looks
like a design mistake, but it save lives
every single day. If you use a normal
bowl to catch flutes from a patient,
you'll make a massive mess because there
is always a gap. But this specific
curves hugs the human body perfectly,
catching every drop without a single
spill. But it's not just for catching
flutes. It is used to carry surgical
tools as well. It has this curve which
acts as a built-in ergonomic handle. It
fits the natural grip of a hand
perfectly, allowing a doctor to carry it
securely with one hand from any angle.
It's perfectly balanced to stay level
even with the heavy tools, so the other
hands stay free for the actual work.
Good design is invisible. To stay for
more.
